assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate (11/13); overall eval pass rate 96% (54/56). Repetitive Test Detection: N/A. Test Documentation: N/A. 1 eval failure sub-task created for eval-3 regression failures |' -- Test Quality is WARN, Eval Quality component is WARN due to eval-3's 2 failing assertions.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 27-31 show Eval Result Detection section: 'An eval result review was detected (review ID 40001): Author: github-actions[bot] -- matches, Body contains ## Eval Results: yes -- matches, Body contains sdlc-workflow/run-evals: yes -- matches, Conclusion: All 3 criteria match; this is a valid eval result review'. Lines 35-42 show extracted eval metrics table with per-eval pass rates and overall 54/56 (96%).

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md is titled '# Review Comment Classification: Comment 50001' and identifies 'Author: reviewer-b (human reviewer)'. Classification is 'suggestion' with reasoning based on reviewer language ('Consider adding'). The file includes a Convention Upgrade Eligibility section but contains zero references to eval detection, eval metrics, eval results, or pass rates. The comment is processed entirely as a normal review comment.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 Test Quality row includes 'eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate (11/13); overall eval pass rate 96% (54/56)'. Lines 44-52 detail the two failing assertions: (1) 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)' and (2) 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path'. Both assertions reference convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md exists and its Description (lines 7-12) states 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures related to convention upgrade eligibility evaluation and sub-task creation'. The Review Context section (lines 33-47) quotes both failing assertion texts and their evidence. report.md line 56 confirms: 'Eval failure sub-task for eval-3: Fix eval-3 assertion failures: convention upgrade eligibility, sub-task creation (labels: ai-generated-jira, eval-failure)'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md lines 33-47 contain '## Review Context' section that quotes: Assertion 1 about convention upgrade eligibility with Evidence 1 stating 'no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented in the classification reasoning', and Assertion 2 about sub-task creation with Evidence 2 stating 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md lines 4-5: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747' -- exact URL match.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | Investigated eval-3 assertion failures; root cause is missing convention upgrade eligibility evaluation in classification pipeline |' -- verdict is 'DONE', not 'N/A'. Lines 64-66 provide detailed root-cause analysis identifying 'a gap in the convention upgrade pipeline' and classifying it as 'a method-based skill gap in the implement-task phase'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only: report.md, subtask-1.md, review-50001.md, and criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md. All are verification/classification/report files. No source code files (.ts, .js, .py, .rs, .go, etc.) or skill definition files (.md in plugins/) appear in the outputs. The report evaluates the existing PR diff without making code changes.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md: Scope Containment (line 7) is 'PASS | All changes confined to the 2 files listed in task: style-conventions.md and SKILL.md' -- compares diff against task's Files to Modify with no eval references. Sensitive Patterns (line 10) is 'PASS | No credentials, secrets, or sensitive data in diff' -- scans diff for secrets with no eval references. Acceptance Criteria (line 12) is 'PASS | 7 of 7 criteria met' and criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md each evaluate a specific criterion against the PR diff content (e.g., criterion-1.md checks for new symbol scanning in style-conventions.md) with zero references to eval results.
